question: <statistical method>
description: Extract all statistical methods explicitly reported in the Association Test Method column. If multiple methods are reported in one study, split them into separate entries.

question: <variables>
description: Extract all variables explicitly reported in the Independent Variable Unit and Dependent Variable Unit columns.

answers.variable: Biomass C (estimates of above and below-ground biomass)
Richness Index (number of species per cell)
Threatened Index (number of threatened species per cell)
Restricted range index (number of restricted-range species per cell)

question: <independent variables>
description: Extract all independent variables explicitly reported in the Independent Variable Unit column.

answers.independent_variable: Biomass C (estimates of above and below-ground biomass)

question: <dependent variables>
description: Extract all dependent variables explicitly reported in the Dependent Variable Unit column.

answers.dependent_variable: Richness Index (number of species per cell)
Threatened Index (number of threatened species per cell)
Restricted range index (number of restricted-range species per cell)

question: <variables> <scale> <unit>
description: Extract all variables together with their explicitly reported measurement scales and units.

answers.unit: percentage contribution of groundwater to base flows
carbon storage potentials for different vegetation types
function of soil depth and leaf litter accumulation index
function of vegetation cover and erodibility
number of vegetation types per QDS

estimate the amount of carbon sequestered each year
pollutant removal service evaluation focused on nitrogen
pollutant removal service evaluation focused on phosphorus

description: Extract measurement scales and units for independent variables only.

question: <dependent variable> <scale> <unit>
description: Extract measurement scales and units for dependent variables only.

question: <independent variable> <dependent variable>
description: Extract all explicitly reported independent-dependent variable pairs. Each pair should be listed once per study, regardless of the number of statistical methods used.

question: <independent variable> <dependent variable> <statistical method>
description: Extract all atomic relationships between independent variables and dependent variables, each paired with a single explicitly reported statistical method. If the same variable pair is analyzed with multiple methods, extract multiple records.

question: <independent variable> <dependent variable> <statistical method> <conditions> <effect size>
description: Extract all reported effect sizes with IV, DV, method, and conditions.

question: average <effect size>
description: Average of all reported effect sizes.
